title: Step 1 – Provide the Context in a PID
overview: The first section of a PID captures the business drivers, problem statement, project purpose, and strategic goals. This context aligns the team and stakeholders from the outset.
detailed_summary: Step 1 of creating a Project Initiation Document is to provide the context. This involves describing why the client is pursuing the project, the problem it aims to solve, and the overall purpose of the work. The writer should also capture any technical drivers versus broader organizational strategy, outline the business goals, define how success will be measured, and include any relevant metrics. A high‑level mission statement or strategic vision helps keep the team focused during solutioning and provides a reference for future enhancements. Gathering this information may require input from the client’s account manager or business sponsor.
content:
Since you’ve been working with the client to scope out the project, chances are you can provide background on some of the business drivers of the project. (if not, ask your Account Manager).

Why is the client pursuing this project?
What’s the problem to be solved?
What’s the project about?

Are there purely technical drivers or is it rooted in the overall organizational strategy?
What are the business goals?
How is success defined?
Are there metrics defined that will measure success in the end?

Outline the strategic vision, goals & objectives and ideally include a high‑level mission statement. This will help align the team on the approach and keep these goals in mind during solutioning. It will also help in defining additional work and potential project enhancements as the team keep this context in mind.
search_queries: what context to include in a project initiation document
how to write the context section of a PID
project purpose and problem statement PID
project goals and success metrics PID
common_questions: What should be included in the context section of a PID?
How do I capture the business drivers for a project?
Do I need to include technical drivers in the PID?
What is a good way to write a mission statement for a PID?
How can I define success metrics in the PID?
can_answer: What context belongs in a PID?
How to describe business drivers in a PID?
best_practices: Include both business and technical drivers to give a full picture.
Ask the account manager or sponsor for missing background information.
warnings: Do not skip the context section; lacking it can cause misalignment later.
